name: Neutron - Spokane
verification_evidence: Neutron colocation lists 422 W Riverside Ave, Suite 1401, Spokane WA 99201 (datacenters.com). Commercial listings (LoopNet/CommercialCafe) confirm 422 W Riverside = the US Bank Building, a 16-story historic (1910) office tower, 180,698 sqft. Suite 1401 = 14th floor, consistent with a 16-story tower. In Esri imagery the tower is identifiable at the address by its long SE-cast shadow (only high-rise on the block); I moved the point off the road/intersection where the Census geocode (47.658011,-117.418417) landed onto the tower rooftop centroid ~47.6583,-117.4191. CANDIDATE not VERIFIED because Neutron is an interior colo tenant inside a multi-tenant office tower, so there is no external data-center signature (no rooftop chillers/substation) visible to independently confirm the facility from imagery.

name: EdgeConneX Hillsboro (EDCPOR02)
verification_evidence: EdgeConneX POR02 (EDCPOR02) is a small (~3 MW) unmarked edge data center listed at '6397 NE Evergreen Pkwy, Bldg A, Hillsboro OR 97124' (datacenterHawk), but other sources give '6297 NE Evergreen Pkwy, Bldg A' and the Census geocoder only matched '6397 NW EVERGREEN PKWY' (45.5505,-122.9101) - a different quadrant/street. Esri imagery of the area (AmberGlen/Evergreen corporate park) shows numerous near-identical flat-roofed office/flex buildings with no distinct data-center signature (no unmistakable rooftop cooling array, substation, or fencing) to single one out. Conflicting street numbers (6397 vs 6297) and quadrant (NE vs NW) plus multiple look-alike candidate buildings make building-level assignment impossible without guessing. Coords left null.
